title: 10.-Amendment to Ground Lease with East Bay Regional Park District at 400 Gilman Street (Tom Bates Regional Sports Complex)

sections.recommendation:
Adopt first reading of an Ordinance authorizing the City Manager to execute an
amendment to the Ground Lease with East Bay Regional Park District for the Tom
Bates Regional Sports Complex at 400 Gilman Street extending the term to December
31, 2053 and includes improvements to be constructed by the City.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
There are no direct fiscal impacts associated with the execution of the proposed
amendment to the Ground Lease.
sections.current_situation:
In 2007, the City entered into a Ground Lease with East Bay Regional Park District
(EBRPD), who owns the property at 400 Gilman Street, to develop and operate what
would become the Tom Bates Regional Sports Complex for a period of 25 years. The
City completed construction of the sports complex in 2008, initially including two artificial
turf fields and parking areas. In 2009 and 2010, additional lease amendments expanded
the leased area and authorized the City to develop additional sports fields. The Tom
Bates Regional Sports Complex has since become one of the most highly used sports
facilities in the region, serving around 19,000 youth and adult athletes every year.
This proposed amendment extends the lease term to 2053, and the City would continue
to have an option to extend the Ground Lease up to an additional 25 years upon mutual
agreement. The amendment also incorporates the improvements planned to the Sports
Complex, including restrooms, a meeting facility, a storage room, up to eight pickleball
courts, and a grass youth soccer field. These improvements advance the City’s
Strategic Plan goal of providing state-of-the-art, well-maintained infrastructure,
amenities, and facilities.
On February 7, 2023, the East Bay Regional Park District Board of Directors approved
this lease amendment, (Reso. No. 2023-02-025, attached). The City Manager needs
Council authorization to sign the lease amendment.

sections.background:
In 2003, Council adopted Resolution No. 62,291-N.S. to execute a joint powers
agreement (JPA) between the City of Berkeley and the cities of Albany, El Cerrito,
Emeryville, and Richmond to develop and operate sports fields on what would become
the Tom Bates Regional Sports Complex on the property at 400 Gilman Street owned
by the East Bay Regional Park District (EBRPD).
In 2004, Council adopted Resolution No. 62,736-N.S. to execute JPA No. 2, which
names the City of Berkeley as the project lead for the “Gilman Project”; and Resolution
No. 62,737-N.S. to execute a Cooperative Funding Agreement between the City and
EBRPD to enable the City to design and construct the sports fields and facilities. In
2007, Council adopted Ordinance No. 6,978-N.S., where the City entered a 25-year
Ground Lease with EBRPD for the 400 Gilman Street property. The Ground Lease
allowed the City to start construction of the Tom Bates Regional Sports Complex, and to
operate and maintain the facility once developed.
The Ground Lease has since been amended twice in 2009 and 2010. These
amendments added 11,027 square feet of land to the Premises and approved additional
sports field development.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
The Ground Lease term extension is categorically exempt from California
Environmental Quality Act (CEQA) requirements for preparing and processing
environmental documentation pursuant to Section 15301 of State CEQA Guidelines,
which in part exempts the leasing of existing public facilities.
On March 25, 2025, Council adopted Resolution No. 71,707-N.S., which adopts the
Initial Study/Mitigated Negative Declaration and the Mitigation Monitoring and Reporting
Plan for the Tom Bates Regional Sports Complex Restroom and Community Space
Project, in accordance with CEQA.
sections.rationale:
This project will add much-needed facilities in the Sports Complex, such as restrooms, a
meeting facility, a storage room, up to eight pickleball courts, and a grass youth soccer
field.
sections.alternatives:
None
CONTACT PERSON
Scott Ferris, Director, Parks Recreation and Waterfront, 981-6700

ORDINANCE NO. -N.S.
AMENDMENT TO THE GROUND LEASE AT 400 GILMAN STREET (TOM BATES
REGIONAL SPORTS COMPLEX)
BE IT ORDAINED by the Council of the City of Berkeley as follows:
Section 1. FINDINGS:
1. City Council adopted Ordinance No. 6,978-N.S. on May 24, 2007, which
authorized the City Manager to enter into a Ground Lease with East Bay
Regional Park District for real property located at 400 Gilman Street, also known
as the Tom Bates Regional Sports Complex.
2. The City and East Bay Regional Park District executed an Amendment to the
Ground Lease dated April 20, 2009, and a second Amendment to the Ground
Lease dated June 30, 2010. These amendments added 11,027 square feet of
land to the Premises and approved additional sports field development.
3. The East Bay Regional Park District adopted Ordinance No. 2023-02-025 on
February 7, 2023, which authorized the agency’s General Manager to amend the
Ground Lease as set forth in Exhibit A.
4. The City will construct improvements on the property, which include a meeting
room, bathrooms, plaza, storage room, up to eight pickleball courts, a grass
youth soccer field, and associated ramps, stairs, fences, and gates. Construction
of these improvements may not commence until the Amendment to the Ground
Lease is executed by the City Manager.
Section 2. AUTHORIZATION FOR CITY MANAGER TO EXECUTE AN AMENDMENT
TO THE GROUND LEASE AT 400 GILMAN STREET.
The City Manager is hereby authorized to execute an amendment to the Ground Lease
with East Bay Regional Park District for real property located at 400 Gilman Street, also
known as the Tom Bates Regional Sports Complex. Such amendment shall be on
substantially the terms set forth in Exhibit A.
Section 3. Copies of this Ordinance shall be posted for two days prior to adoption in the
display case located near the wa
